data.title: Upstart secures up to $1.2B loan purchase deal with Centerbridge
data.description:
• Upstart Holdings (UPST) on Wednesday said it has entered into a multi-year agreement with funds managed by Centerbridge Partners, under which the investment firm will purchase up to $1.2 billion of consumer loans originated on Upstart’s platform.
  • The deal is structured as a 24-month forward-flow arrangement and follows an initial transaction between the two parties in 2024.
  • Upstart said the agreement is aimed at expanding its funding capacity and supporting loan originations through its artificial intelligence-based lending marketplace.
  • UPST +3.82% premarket to $34.47.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Vulcan Value Partners Small Cap Portfolio adds RYAN, TRU; exits CCK, VRTS among Q1 moves
data.description:
In the first quarter of 2026, Vulcan Value Partners Small Cap Portfolio initiated positions in Ryan Specialty (RYAN), TransUnion (TRU), StandardAero (SARO), and CTS Eventim.

On the other hand, the fund exited five positions during the quarter, including Virtus Investment Partners (VRTS), Crown Holdings (CCK), The Middleby Corporation (MIDD), The Timken Company (TKR), and Sealed Air Corporation (SEE), to reallocate capital into more discounted businesses.

Source: Q1 fund letter

data.title: Kopin wins $3.2M FPV drone market order, targets 40K goggles by 2028
data.description:
• Kopin (KPON) announced on Wednesday a $3.2M initial order for its new optical module, marking its entry into the fast-growing FPV drone market.
  • The deal has scale potential, with deliveries of up to 40K FPV goggles by 2028.
  • Its new Sentinel FPV tech enables dual situational awareness (see drone feed + surroundings), making it a high-value defense-focused upgrade.
  • The company is increasing production and looking for more partnerships, pointing to future growth potential.
  • The stock is trading ~6.67% higher at ~$3.04 in premarket trading.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Alpha secures $31.9M financing backed by Nvidia B300 hardware assets
data.description:
• Alpha Compute (ALP) has inked a binding term sheet for a $31.9M non-recourse loan facility secured by B300 Nvidia (NVDA) graphics processing units (GPUs).
  • The transaction marks the company's strategy to scale its AI compute capabilities while optimizing its capital structure, it said.
  • Proceeds from the facility are expected to be used for AI infrastructure/data center expansion/networking, supporting Alpha Compute's long-term growth initiatives.
  • ALP +18.18% premarket to $0.33.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Outlook Therapeutics announces $5M registered direct offering priced at-the-market
data.description:
• Outlook Therapeutics (OTLK) on Wednesday said it has entered into an agreement for the purchase and sale of an aggregate of 16.12M shares of its common stock at an offering price of $0.31 per share of common stock in a registered direct offering priced at-the-market under Nasdaq rules. 
  • OTLK -12.74% premarket to $0.2787.
  • Additionally, in a concurrent private placement, the company will issue unregistered warrants to purchase up to an aggregate of 16.12M shares of common stock at an exercise price of $0.31 per share.
  • The closing of the offering is expected to occur on or about April 23, 2026.
  • The aggregate gross proceeds to the company from the offering are expected to be approximately $5 million. 
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Moody's Q1 earnings beat, helped by strong issuance environment
data.description:
Moody's (MCO) stock rose 1.4% in Wednesday premarket trading after the ratings and market analytics company's Q1 earnings exceeded the Wall Street consensus on strong results in its investor services and analytics divisions.

Q1 adjusted EPS of $4.33, topping the average analyst estimate of $4.22, rose from $3.64 in Q4 and $3.83 in last year's Q1.

Q1 revenue of $2.08B, beating the $2.06B consensus, increased from $1.89B in the prior quarter and $1.92B a year ago.

"MIS (Moody's Investor Services) achieved record revenues of $1.2B on over $2T in rated issuance and delivered an adjusted operating margin of 67%," said President and CEO Rob Fauber. "MA (Moody's Analytics) continued its growth momentum with 8% ARR (annualized recurring revenue) growth and 250 basis points of adjusted operating margin expansion. As AI adoption accelerates, it is driving demand for Moody’s decision-grade connected intelligence in high-stakes environments."

The company reaffirmed its 2026 guidance for revenue growth in the high-single-digit percent range and adjusted EPS of $16.40-$17.00.

Analytics revenue of $926M dropped from $943M in Q4 and climbed from $859M in last year's Q1. The division's 8% Y/Y growth includes 7% growth in Decision Solutions, an 8% increase in Research and Insights, and a 10% improvement in Data and Information. Recurring revenue of $909M increased 11% on a reported basis and 7% on an organic constant currency basis.

Investors Service revenue of $1.15B, marking the highest quarter on record, climbed from $946M in the previous quarter and $1.07B a year ago. The 8% Y/Y growth was driven by a 33% increase in investment grade revenue, a 31% jump in high-yield revenue, and corporate finance revenue growth of 12%. Leveraged  loans revenue dipped 13% Y/Y.

Expenses of $1.16B, vs. the Visible Alpha estimate of $1.10B, rose from $1.12B in Q4 and $1.08B in Q1 2025.

Adjusted operating margin of 53.2% compared with 48.7% in the prior quarter and 51.7% a year ago.

Conference call at 9:00 AM.

data.title: Citi's Wealth unit launches AI assistant in partnership with Google Cloud, DeepMind
data.description:
Citigroup (C) on Wednesday said its Wealth unit has launched an AI assistant that was built using technology from Google Cloud and Google DeepMind.

The new capability will be made available to Citigold clients from this summer in a phased roll-out.

Citi Sky is expected to create a more intuitive, responsive, and personalized wealth experience for the Wealth unit clients and advisors.

"For decades, managing your financial life meant navigating apps, calls, and meetings. With Citi Sky, you simply ask – and act. This is the shift from interface to intelligence, from transactions to outcomes," said Andy Sieg, head of Wealth.

"It doesn't replace our advisors – it makes them more powerful, extending their reach and deepening their impact. In fact, Citi Wealth plans to add advisors in the years ahead," said Sieg.

"With Gemini Enterprise as the backbone of Citi Sky, combined with frontier models from Google DeepMind, Citi Wealth is establishing a new blueprint for how agentic AI can drive high-quality, personalized financial insights for millions of its customers," said Google Cloud CEO Thomas Kurian.

data.title: Honeywell says Quantinuum unit confidentially filed IPO
data.description:
• Honeywell (HON) said on Wednesday that its Quantinuum quantum computing unit confidentially filed for an initial public offering on Feb. 17.
  • The number of shares to be offered and the price range for the proposed offering have not yet been determined, Honeywell added.
  • Honeywell shares rose 1.7% in premarket trading, while other quantum computing stocks, such as IonQ (IONQ), D-Wave Quantum (QBTS), Quantum Computing (QUBT), and Arqit Quantum (ARQQ), were mixed.
  • Based in Charlotte, N.C., Honeywell said in January that it intended to submit an S-1 filing with the Securities and Exchange Commission for its quantum computing unit, Quantinuum, and have it go public.
  • Quantinuum raised $600M in 2025 at a $10B valuation. The unit previously raised $300M in January 2024 in a round led by JPMorgan Chase, which valued it at $5B before the deal.

data.title: Rheinmetall wins billion-euro Bundeswehr drone contract
data.description:
Germany’s Rheinmetall (RNMBF) (RNMBY) secured a major contract from the Bundeswehr to supply large volumes of FV-014 loitering munition systems, reinforcing investor expectations for sustained growth in Europe’s fast-expanding defense market.

The framework agreement is valued in the billion-euro range, with an initial order worth about €300 million gross, according to an announcement Wednesday. Rheinmetall (RNMBF) (RNMBY) said the contract will be booked in April 2026, providing a near-term boost to its already strong order backlog.

Deliveries are scheduled to begin in the first half of 2027, while qualification of the system is expected to start in the second quarter of 2026. The agreement also includes an option for a five-digit number of additional autonomous reconnaissance and strike drones.

For investors, the announcement highlights Rheinmetall’s (RNMBF) (RNMBY) growing position in one of defense’s hottest segments: low-cost autonomous weapons systems shaped by lessons from modern warfare in Ukraine and elsewhere. Demand for loitering munitions has surged as militaries seek precision strike capability without relying solely on traditional missiles or manned aircraft.

Chief Executive Armin Papperger said the company had developed the autonomous drone in a short period of time and would soon ramp production to industrial scale with high output volumes.

The FV-014 has a range of up to 100 kilometers, carries a warhead of roughly 4 kilograms and can remain airborne for as long as 70 minutes while observing targets before striking. Rheinmetall said development and production will be carried out entirely within the European Union.

That EU-based supply chain could appeal to European governments seeking strategic autonomy and faster procurement cycles as geopolitical tensions drive higher defense spending across NATO.

Rheinmetall (RNMBF) (RNMBY) has emerged as one of the clearest corporate beneficiaries of Europe’s rearmament push, and this latest order suggests that unmanned systems may become an increasingly meaningful contributor to revenue growth beyond its traditional ammunition, vehicles and artillery businesses.

data.title: Aclarion announces $2.5M stock repurchase plan
data.description:
• Aclarion (ACON) on Wednesday announced a share repurchase program of up to $2.5 million of the company’s outstanding common stock.
  • Aclarion expects to execute the repurchase program over the next 12 months. 
  • The company intends to fund the repurchase program using existing cash and cash equivalents.
  • As of March 31, 2026, the company had cash and cash equivalents of approximately $19.0 million.
  • ACON +4.81% premarket to $3.7.
  • Source: Press Release
